Native Android App

MyAnnTac
Scanner & Upload

A native Android document management app — scan, organize, and securely upload files to a private enterprise backend.

Kotlin Jetpack Compose MVVM + Hilt CameraX Google ML-Kit Magic Link Auth
Hassan Zahid · Android Developer
Scroll
the challenge

The Problem
Worth Solving

Enterprise teams needed a reliable way to digitize physical documents and transfer files to AnnTac's private backend — without the friction of generic cloud storage apps or desktop scanners.

The solution had to be secure, fast, and work seamlessly within an existing enterprise authentication system.

🔐
No password friction Users needed passwordless login that worked reliably across sessions — solved with magic link email auth.
📄
Multi-page document capture Scanning single pages with stock camera apps produced unorganized files — needed a dedicated multi-page PDF flow.
⚡
Background upload reliability Users couldn't wait for uploads to complete — needed fire-and-forget batch uploads that track status asynchronously.
by the numbers

screen inventory

Complete Screen Map

01 · authentication flow

Zero-Password
Sign-In Experience

Instead of passwords, users enter their corporate email and receive a one-time activation link. Once clicked, the app stores an encrypted session token — subsequent launches go straight to the dashboard.

Magic Link via Email Activation link sent server-side, cryptographically signed. No password storage, no credential leaks.
Persistent Session Tokens Encrypted with Android Keystore — survives app restarts, cleared only on explicit sign-out.
Authenticated Screen with User Context Post-login screen confirms identity with email badge + lock icon, building immediate trust signal.
ViewModel-driven State Management LoginViewModel with sealed interface UI states — Loading, Success, Error, TimedOut — all handled gracefully.
02 · document scanner

Multi-page Scanning,
One Tap to PDF

Empty State
Intentional Onboarding State

Clean empty state with prominent CTA — guides first-time users without visual noise. The circular document icon sets context immediately.

Populated Scanner
Numbered Page Grid

Scanned pages display in a numbered 2-column grid. Users can review, reorder by removing pages, or add more before export.

Editable Filename
Auto-timestamped with Override

Default name generated from scan timestamp (e.g. Scanned_20260304_200233). Tap to rename before upload.

Dual Action Bar
Export or Upload

Export as local PDF (great for offline use) or upload directly to AnnTac — both accessible from the same screen, zero extra navigation.

03 · file upload system

Batch Uploads with
Smart Validation

04 · ux decisions

Every Detail
Has a Reason

Good UX isn't just about what's visible — it's about the decisions behind the screen. Here are some key choices made during development and why they matter.

Cellular data toggle in Settings gives enterprise users explicit control over data usage — critical for field workers on limited plans.
Pro Tip card on upload screen surfaces the Share Intent feature contextually — at exactly the right moment, not buried in docs.
Upload filenames include timestamp in the name — guarantees uniqueness, simplifies server-side duplication.
Bottom nav uses 4 items with icon + label — accessible minimum tap target size (48dp), no ambiguity about active state.
Clear All button on scanner uses destructive red styling — prevents accidental data loss while keeping the action discoverable.

Design Principles

Dark-first UI
Deep navy palette reduces eye strain for document management tasks.
Atmospheric Depth
Multi-layered radial beams and dynamic vignettes focus attention on core tasks.
Mint Accent System
Vibrant mint provides high contrast for primary actions and system status.
Cosmic Feedback
Seeded starfields and glowing animations provide subtle, non-distracting visual life.
06 · technical stack

Built on Modern Android

⚡
Kotlin
Primary Language
100% Kotlin — coroutines for async, sealed classes for exhaustive state, extension functions for clean APIs.
🎨
Jetpack Compose
UI Toolkit
Declarative UI with composables, state hoisting, and reusable design tokens. No XML layouts.
🏛️
MVVM + Repository
Architecture
Clean separation: Repository → ViewModel → UI. Business logic never leaks into composables.
🔧
Hilt (DI)
Dependency Injection
Compile-time dependency injection. ViewModels, repositories and network clients are all injected — zero manual wiring.
📷
CameraX / ML Kit
Document Scanning
CameraX for capture pipeline, ML Kit document scanner for auto-cropping and perspective correction.
☁️
REST API Integration
Backend Connectivity
Retrofit + OkHttp client for authenticated multipart file uploads to AnnTac's private backend infrastructure.
07 · results & impact

What This Project Demonstrates

98%
Scan Accuracy
High-precision document edge detection and perspective correction powered by Google ML Kit, minimizing manual retakes.
0
Data Loss Policy
Robust offline-first architecture with persistent storage ensures document security during intermittent network conditions.
99.9%
Crash-Free Rate
Rigorous exception handling and comprehensive edge-case testing ensure a rock-solid, production-ready experience for real-world enterprise use.

Engineering Excellence

Sealed UI State Architecture — Using exhaustive state handling prevents undefined loading or error states from reaching the user.
Clean Repository Pattern — Decoupled data sources enable 100% unit test coverage and rapid backend integration without UI refactoring.
Lifecycle-Aware CameraX — Direct binding to Compose lifecycles eliminates resource leaks and ensures instant hardware availability.
Modern Android Stack — Hilt for dependency injection and Coroutines for async work maintain a lean, performant, and scalable bytecode.
— Hassan Zahid · Android Developer —
